# Shivesh Sood

> Product Lead, Ex-Founder and Builder. Applying emerging technologies to solve complex problems across AI, SaaS, Agriculture, and more.

## About

Shivesh Sood is a Lead AI Product Manager at Harvard Business School Foundry in Cambridge, MA. He specializes in identifying problems, prototyping solutions, leading cross-functionally, and communicating solutions effectively. He has experience across both hardware and software domains.

## Current Role

- Lead AI Product Manager at Harvard Business School Foundry (2024–Present)
- Led user research, design and product for Agentic AI products
- Shipped 10+ AI agents and an AI avatar coach with 60% repeat usage

## Education

- Master's in Engineering, Design & Business, Harvard University (2024) — Data Science & AI, Product Management & Strategy, User Research & Design
- Bachelor's in Electronics Engineering, M.S. Ramaiah Institute of Technology

## Experience

- Harvard Business School Foundry — Lead AI Product Manager (2024–Present)
- Atomic — Summer Associate, New Products (2023) — $700M+ venture studio, built concepts across ClimateTech, Generative AI, and B2B SaaS
- StayQrious (YC) — Product Manager (2020–2021) — First product hire, launched 50+ features, drove $1M+ ARR
- Collins Aerospace — Software Engineer (2019–2020) — Cryptographic libraries with 1000x speedup, 2 patents filed
- Hyperbloom Agritech — Founder & Product Lead (2017–2019) — Edge AI hardware for farmers, incubated at IIM Bangalore

## Skills

Product Management, Product Strategy, Agentic AI, LLM Evaluation, Python, UI/UX Design, Full-Stack Web Development, Data Science, Figma, User Research, GTM Strategy

## Selected Projects

- MovieBuff (2026) — AI-powered infinite movie discovery canvas
- Thunder (2024) — Climate data framework and Spatial AI engine for emissions tracking
- Indigenous Textiles Around the World (2022) — Data visualization with Python, Keras, and image recognition
- StayQrious Webinar Product (2021) — Webinar platform supporting 5000+ concurrent attendees
- SWARAF (2018–2019) — Precision agriculture tools for smallholder farmers in India
